input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>Two bare feet rest on a lined notebook. The right foot grips a yellow pencil with a blue eraser between the big and second toes. The left foot lies flat nearby. White text overlay reads "JiMMY KHaN- FooT WriTer". The toes flex slightly, adjusting the pencil. A grey textured surface appears at the top.</think><action>silent</action>

   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text:
[0-2] Two bare feet rest on a lined notebook. The right foot grips a yellow pencil with a blue eraser between the big and second toes; the left foot lies flat nearby. A grey textured surface appears at the top. White text "JiMMY KHaN- FooT WriTer" overlays the scene.
[2-6] The feet separate; the left foot now grips a hollow orange cap between toes. The right foot holds the pencil vertically with the tip pointing up. The left foot moves inward, positioning the cap over the pencil's tip and sliding it onto the point held steady by the right foot's toes.
[6-14] The feet separate widely across the page. The left foot moves to the far left edge, gripping the hollow orange cap. The right foot shifts to the right side, holding the yellow pencil vertically with its tip resting on the lined paper. It presses down to create small blue marks and writes visible blue ink characters. The text overlay persists against the grey background.

[14-28] The right foot grips a yellow pencil with a blue eraser between the toes, writing blue Urdu characters on lined white paper. The script extends from right to left as the foot maneuvers the pencil to form words. Simultaneously, the left foot remains stationary at the top left edge, anchoring the paper by gripping a hollow orange cap between the toes. The white text overlay "JiMMY KHaN- FooT WriTer" persists at the top against the grey concrete background throughout the sequence. The writing action continues steadily, adding characters to the existing blue ink text.

[28-40] The right foot maneuvers a yellow pencil with a blue tip to inscribe blue Urdu characters on lined paper, extending the text line further to the left. The left foot remains fixed at the top left edge, gripping an orange pen cap between its toes to anchor the notebook steady against the grey surface. The white overlay text "JiMMY KHaN- FooT WriTer" persists at the top. [40-42] The right foot shifts position below the main line of script to write the number "17" in blue ink. The left foot maintains its hold on the orange cap to secure the paper as the numerals are formed.

The left foot remains stationary at the top left edge, gripping the orange cap to secure the notebook. The white text overlay persists at the top against the grey background.

[44-46] The right foot writes additional blue Urdu characters next to the number "17" on the lined paper, extending the lower line of text. The toes manipulate the yellow pencil to form the script while the left foot remains stationary at the top left, gripping the orange cap to secure the notebook against the grey surface.

[46-48] The right foot writes additional blue Urdu script below the primary line, completing characters next to the number '17'. The yellow pencil remains gripped between the toes during this action. The left foot stays fixed at the top left corner, clutching the orange cap to stabilize the notebook on the grey background.

[48-50] The right foot continues writing blue Urdu script on the second line below the main sentence. The yellow pencil forms characters at the end of this lower line. The left foot stays stationary at the top left edge, holding the orange cap to keep the lined paper steady on the grey background.

[50-52] The right foot continues inscribing blue Urdu script on the second line below the primary sentence. The yellow pencil moves to form additional characters, extending the text line further left. The left foot remains fixed at the top left corner, holding the orange cap to stabilize the notebook on the grey background.
